change_summary: Decider commit 940be96 applied 3 patches per c5 review: (1) replaced first-principles overclaim in detail_verdict_text opening with CMB-seismic-boundary-conditions framing + 3:1-not-first-principles caveat (ISS-2200 fix); (2) replaced CHAOS-7 strawman in detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict with dilemma framing 'no published source ... no derivation from canonical parameters' (ISS-2201 fix on detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict surfaces); (3) inserted aetheric-flow axis-vs-magnitude clarification sentence in detail_evidence (c5 H3 fix). EXP-433 created in expansion-tracker (status pending, review_class deep-attack) for defense_survives=3 neutralization. Patches landed cleanly; build+test passed (3692/3692).
our_argument_summary: WIN-063 claims a 3:1 hemispheric magnetic decay asymmetry ratio confirms the dome model. Our review now argues (post-c5 patches) (a) the QUALITATIVE asymmetric decay is reproduced by MHD geodynamo simulations from independently measured CMB heat-flux boundary conditions (Aubert et al. 2013) — a four-domain chain (seismic → CMB → MHD → magnetic obs) the dome lacks; (b) the SPECIFIC 3:1 ratio is not a first-principles prediction in either model, so the dome's defect is the absence of any derivation path from {disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, moon_altitude} to specifically 3:1 (vs 2:1 or 5:1) — the dilemma forecloses both post-hoc adoption and assertion-by-fiat; (c) toroidal-aetheric-flow asymmetry-in-axis ≠ asymmetry-in-magnitude, and no rule connects flow chirality to 3:1; (d) SAA longitudinal specificity contradicts dome axisymmetric H(r); (e) post-hoc carve-out fails (CHAOS-7 published 2020, IGRF-14 2024, dome adopts V51-era). Verdict: Std Model Explains.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py snapshot in raw-text/ corpus contains an asymmetry-ratio domain or fetch for this WIN; the dome paragraph specifies 3:1 vs globe ~1.0x but provides no formula relating any canonical dome parameter to 3:1.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No automation surface — the WIN exists as a written claim only.
data_sources_used: implicit reliance on CHAOS-7 / IGRF for the 3:1 figure though dome paragraph does not cite either by name
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome paragraph never specifies (a) source for 3:1, (b) averaging window, (c) flat-disc-vs-globe hemispheric definition, (d) mechanism. Each unspecified parameter is an implicit escape clause: any observed ratio can be made to fit by selecting a window/definition retroactively.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — our code_analysis_tags accurately reflect the absence of any monitor surface for this WIN and the absence of any derivation path from dome geometry to 3:1.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py snapshot in raw-text/ corpus contains an asymmetry-ratio domain or fetch for this WIN. Verdict text appropriately hedged: "no asymmetry-ratio domain appears in any monitor.py snapshot in our raw-text/ corpus". c5 page-hash byte-identical for 34 consecutive polls remains corroborating evidence that no recent monitor-surface change has occurred for this WIN.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Hemispheric/SAA decay asymmetry is described and explained in peer-reviewed literature: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020, doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9); Olson & Deguen 2012 (doi:10.1038/ngeo1506); Aubert et al. 2013 (doi:10.1038/nature12574). The dome renames the cause (toroidal aetheric return-path through SAA shoulder) without quantitative derivation. No change since c5.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 published 2020; IGRF-14 cycle published 2024. Dome WIN-063 dates from V51-era (2026-04 cluster). Indexed public datasets predate dome adoption — fails Part 3.1b legitimate-novelty carve-out. No timestamped pre-registration found. No change since c5.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site maps any of {disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, sun_altitude=5,733 km, moon_altitude=2,534 km} to specifically 3:1 (rather than 2:1 or 5:1). The dome stated mechanism (sub-terrestrial return path through SAA shoulder) is qualitative; H(r) is axisymmetric in longitude; transient sun/moon/aether positions average to azimuthal symmetry over secular timescales. No change since c5.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD parallel-surface gap from c5 Hole 0 / ISS-2201. The decider commit 940be96 fixed the CHAOS-7 strawman wording in detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict (both confirmed in diff), but the SAME strawman wording survives verbatim in tldr_evidence: "The dome's WIN-063 specifies a 3:1 asymmetry ratio but adopts that value from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry". This is the exact attack surface ISS-2120 (c4) and ISS-2201 (c5) were designed to neutralize — the dome's raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt paragraph does NOT cite CHAOS-7 as the source for 3:1 (verified verbatim in c5 review). A dome defender can quote that paragraph alongside our tldr_evidence (high-visibility skim-reader Evidence panel) and accuse us of source-attribution fabrication — the same critique c4 and c5 raised, on a third surface neither cycle audited. The decider's commit message says "Fixed both" of detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict, but ISS-2201 was scoped narrowly and missed tldr_evidence. This is a textbook three-surface-strawman pattern: detail_evidence (fixed c4), detail_verdict_text + tldr_verdict (fixed c5), tldr_evidence (still open at c6).
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the dilemma framing to tldr_evidence. Suggested edit: replace "but adopts that value from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry (no formula connects disc_radius=20,015 km or firmament_height=9,086 km to specifically 3:1)" with "but provides no published source for that specific value and no derivation from dome geometry (no formula connects disc_radius=20,015 km or firmament_height=9,086 km to specifically 3:1)". This brings tldr_evidence into alignment with the dilemma framing already applied to detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and tldr_verdict. Decider should also re-open ISS-2201 (or create a successor ISS) noting the parallel-surface scope was incomplete — tldr_evidence was missed in both the c4 and c5 patch sets.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD parallel-surface gap from c5 Hole 1 / ISS-2200. The decider fixed the first-principles overclaim in detail_verdict_text (confirmed in diff: opening now reads "reproduced by MHD geodynamo simulations from independently measured CMB boundary conditions" and adds "the specific 3:1 ratio is not a first-principles prediction in either model"), but the SAME overclaim survives in tldr_verdict opening: "The asymmetry ratio comes from core fluid dynamics, modeled from first principles." This creates a direct internal contradiction with our own corrected detail_verdict_text, which now explicitly says 3:1 is NOT a first-principles prediction. A dome defender can: (a) quote Aubert et al. 2013's methods section (which is explicit about using seismic-tomography CMB heat-flux maps as inputs, not pure first-principles physics) alongside our TLDR Verdict box, accusing us of the same overclaim Hole 1 flagged; (b) point at the contradiction between our TLDR ("modeled from first principles") and our detail_verdict ("not a first-principles prediction in either model"), accusing us of self-inconsistency. ISS-2200's scope was narrowly detail_verdict_text — the TLDR-surface parallel was missed.
holes_found.recommendation: Edit tldr_verdict opening sentence to remove the overclaim. Suggested edit: replace "The asymmetry ratio comes from core fluid dynamics, modeled from first principles. The dome specifies a 3:1 ratio but provides no published source for that specific value and no derivation from dome geometry — without a prior derivation, any observed asymmetry ratio would retrospectively match." with "The asymmetric decay is reproduced by MHD geodynamo simulations from independently measured CMB boundary conditions, but the specific 3:1 ratio is not a first-principles prediction even in standard physics — the dome problem is the absence of any derivation path from dome geometry to 3:1, so any observed ratio retrospectively matches." Decider should re-open ISS-2200 or create a successor noting parallel-surface scope was incomplete.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD from c5 Hole 2. Olson & Deguen 2012 (doi:10.1038/ngeo1506) is still jointly attributed with Aubert et al. 2013 for using PREM/AK135 + mantle-tomography boundary conditions in detail_evidence. The decider patches did not touch this; c5 marked it low priority and recommended verification of Olson & Deguen 2012 methodology when network access permits. Risk surface unchanged: a defender who reads Olson & Deguen 2012 carefully and finds it uses different methodology than Aubert et al. 2013 could attack the bundled attribution. Low impact since Aubert et al. carries the seismic-inputs argument on its own.
holes_found.recommendation: Defer to next cycle when network access permits DOI resolution, OR attribute the seismic-tomography-input claim only to Aubert et al. 2013 and cite Olson & Deguen 2012 separately for the reversed-flux-patch identification only. No verdict impact.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: ISS-2201 closed by commit 940be96 with scope "detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict". tldr_evidence carries the identical strawman wording and was missed by both c4 (ISS-2120 scoped detail_evidence) and c5 (ISS-2201 scoped detail_verdict_text + tldr_verdict). Decider should re-open ISS-2201 or create a successor ISS for the tldr_evidence fix.

ISS-2200 closed by commit 940be96 with scope "detail_verdict_text opening". tldr_verdict opening sentence retains "modeled from first principles" — same kind of overclaim, on a parallel TLDR surface. Decider should re-open ISS-2200 or create a successor ISS for the tldr_verdict fix.

stronger_arguments.description: The contradiction between tldr_verdict ("modeled from first principles") and detail_verdict_text ("not a first-principles prediction in either model") is itself the strongest argument for the c6 H1 patch: a defender quoting both surfaces side-by-side would have us conceding self-inconsistency without firing a shot. Fix the TLDR and the trap closes.

When tldr_verdict gets patched, the four-domain-chain framing already proven in detail_verdict_text (seismic → CMB → MHD → magnetic obs) is a candidate one-sentence summary for the TLDR. It compresses the discriminator without invoking the disputable "first principles" wording.

kernel_of_truth.description: The c5 review (monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-063.c5.json:advocate_mode.defense_survives=3) correctly identifies that toroidal-aetheric-flow geometry IS qualitatively asymmetric in axis (poloidal vs toroidal). The decider's c5 H3 patch to detail_evidence (commit 940be96 diff: 'Even if a toroidal-flow geometry is inherently asymmetric in axis (poloidal vs toroidal)') credits this kernel — we don't deny that toroidal flow has asymmetric structure, only that axis-asymmetry is not magnitude-asymmetry. The dome author has a legitimate observation that flow chirality can be a source of directional structure in geodynamo systems (Aubert et al. 2013, doi:10.1038/nature12574, explicitly models toroidal modes), but the dome's WIN-063 paragraph (raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt) does not publish a quantitative rule connecting flow chirality to a specific decay ratio.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Even granting the kernel — toroidal flow has asymmetric structure — the dome paragraph in raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt publishes no rule connecting flow chirality to specifically 3:1 (rather than 2:1 or 5:1). Our detail_evidence already pre-empts this: 'asymmetry-in-axis is not asymmetry-in-magnitude, and the dome publishes no rule connecting flow chirality to specifically 3:1' (data/wins.json:063.detail_evidence, post-940be96). Without a published rule, the dome can retroactively 'match' any observed ratio; that is the textbook unfalsifiability the verdict text already names. The toroidal-vortex argument is exactly the survival route EXP-433 (monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json:items[EXP-433]) is scoped to neutralize via analyst Mode 3 — kernel acknowledged, claim still does not survive.

deeper_analysis: This is the third cycle in which the same source-attribution strawman bug (the 'adopts from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving from dome geometry' wording) and its conjugate (the 'first-principles' overclaim) have been flagged on different surfaces. c4 (ISS-2120) caught it in detail_evidence and patched there only. c5 (ISS-2201) caught it in detail_verdict_text and tldr_verdict and patched there only. c6 catches it in tldr_evidence (strawman) and tldr_verdict opening (first-principles overclaim) — the last two TLDR surfaces the prior cycles missed. The pattern is a textbook 'partial fix scoped too narrowly' bug, repeated three times across three cycles. Tinker or decider may want to consider whether the patch-review template should explicitly enumerate ALL six text surfaces (claim, finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) and require the curmudgeon or decider to check each one when applying a wording fix — the current pattern of catching surfaces in series means each cycle costs an Opus review pass for what should be a single multi-surface sweep. Despite this surface-coverage gap, the verdict (Std Model Explains) clearly holds: the qualitative phenomenon is reproduced from independent inputs (Aubert et al. 2013), the specific 3:1 is undefined in dome geometry, and the post-hoc test fails on dataset-availability grounds. defense_survives stays at 3 from c5 because EXP-433 (analyst Mode 3, status pending) has not yet landed; once EXP-433 prose lands, expect defense_survives to drop. Confidence raised to 0.85 from c5's 0.80 because the underlying scientific argument is now tighter on the detail surfaces; the open holes are all parallel-surface fixes, not new attacks.

citations_verified: CHAOS-7 / Finlay et al. 2020, doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 (Earth, Planets and Space): verified previously in c3-c5; no change to citation accuracy in c6.
Aubert et al. 2013, doi:10.1038/nature12574 (Nature): verified previously; methods section uses seismic-tomography CMB heat-flux maps as inputs per detail_evidence framing — accurate.
NOAA WMM URL https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model: structurally valid URL, verified in prior cycle.
citations_unchecked: Olson & Deguen 2012, doi:10.1038/ngeo1506 (Nature Geoscience): joint attribution for PREM/AK135 + mantle-tomography boundary conditions not independently verified for Olson & Deguen specifically — carry-forward minor concern from c5 H2.

advocate_mode.weakest_argument: Dome defender best play after the c6 patches land: 'Your own detail_verdict_text now concedes the 3:1 ratio is not a first-principles prediction in either model. Standard physics matches the asymmetry qualitatively but the 3:1 is also not derived first-principles there. Why is our 3:1 worse than your 3:1?' Our pre-empt is the four-domain chain (seismic → CMB → MHD → magnetic obs vs the dome single-domain assertion), but this discriminator could be sharper — the current detail_verdict says 'chain-length / domain-independence (seismic data → CMB conditions → MHD output → magnetic observations is a four-domain chain; the dome has a one-domain assertion)' but only in passing in the c5 H1 patch. EXP-433 (analyst Mode 3) is scoped to address this — the toroidal-aetheric-vortex argument survived c5 because the chain-length discriminator was implicit, not foregrounded.
new_attacks_considered: TLDR self-contradiction (tldr_verdict says "modeled from first principles" but detail_verdict says "not a first-principles prediction") — addressed by c6 H1.
tldr_evidence still carrying the source-attribution strawman wording the dome paragraph does not support — addressed by c6 H0.

Could a defender argue the chain-length discriminator is unprincipled (long chains can be wrong, short chains can be right)? Counter: chain-length matters when each domain contributes independently measurable boundary conditions and the simulation is validated against (not tuned to) the observed quantity. This is detail_evidence text already; could be sharper in EXP-433.

related_wins_checked: WIN-005 (African SAA cell faster than Atlantic cell)
cross_win_consistency.consistency_findings: WIN-063 detail_evidence correctly cross-references WIN-005 as the prior CHAOS-7 asymmetric decay claim and explicitly notes WIN-063 takes the same observation and repackages it as a ratio metric — the re-slicing concern is internally consistent with the family verdicts (WIN-041 Misleading, WIN-042 Misleading). No new cross-WIN inconsistencies introduced by the c5 patches.

numeric_claims_checked.claim: Disc parameters {disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, sun_altitude=5,733 km, moon_altitude=2,534 km}
numeric_claims_checked.verification: Match SESSION-CONTEXT canonical dome parameters; no derivation from any of these to 3:1 found in dome corpus.

numeric_claims_checked.claim: SAA longitude band roughly 30°W to 90°W on the globe
numeric_claims_checked.verification: Approximately correct; SAA extends across southern Atlantic and parts of South America. Mirror image cited as ~150°E is plausible (180° rotation).

numeric_claims_checked.claim: CHAOS-7 published 2020, IGRF-14 cycle published 2024 (5-yr cadence after IGRF-13 2019)
numeric_claims_checked.verification: IGRF cadence is 5 years; IGRF-13 was 2019, so IGRF-14 = 2024 is correct.

summary_for_decider: WIN-063 c6 (qid=405, queue_pushed_at 2026-05-26T01:20:42Z) verifying decider commit 940be96 patches against c5 holes. RESULT: 3 of 4 c5 holes fully fixed (H0 detail_verdict_text + tldr_verdict, H1 detail_verdict_text, H3 aetheric-flow pre-empt). H2 minor citation deferred per c5 recommendation. EXP-433 created in expansion-tracker for defense neutralization. THREE NEW HOLES in c6: (a) MODERATE carry-forward of ISS-2201 — tldr_evidence still has the strawman 'adopts from CHAOS-7 observation' wording; (b) MODERATE carry-forward of ISS-2200 — tldr_verdict opening still says 'modeled from first principles', contradicting our own corrected detail_verdict_text; (c) MINOR carry-forward of c5 H2 Olson & Deguen attribution. Recommended action: re-open ISS-2200 and ISS-2201 (or create successor ISSs) to fix the parallel TLDR surfaces; queue WIN-063 for c7 verification afterwards. Verdict (Std Model Explains) holds. Confidence 0.85 (raised from 0.80). defense_survives stays at 3 pending EXP-433 prose. The three-cycle pattern of partial-fix-on-narrow-surface for the same strawman family suggests tinker should evaluate whether patch templates should require all-surfaces sweeps when wording fixes target shared phrasings. queue_id 405 set; matches queue item.
